---
title: "Session 7 · Interactions & Simple Slopes"
---

## Overview

So far every predictor has had one fixed effect. This session relaxes that assumption with interaction terms, which let the slope of one predictor *depend on* the value of another --- the regression expression of moderation. By adding a product of two predictors and reading off simple slopes, we gain a flexible vocabulary for effects that change across conditions, a key generalization in the course's "specific → general" arc.

## Learning objectives

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

- Add a product of two predictors to a regression to model an interaction.
- Interpret an interaction coefficient as the change in one predictor's slope per unit of the other.
- Compute and report simple slopes at meaningful values of a moderator.
- Distinguish a main (conditional) effect from an interaction effect.
- Explain how moderation is represented by the product of two predictors.

## This session's materials

- {{< fa book-open-reader >}} **Read:** [Chapter 5: Interactions](/notes/chapters/05-interactions.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa laptop-code >}} **Lab:** [Lab 6: Interactions](/labs/labs/lab06-interactions.html){target="_blank"}
- {{< fa clipboard-list >}} **Project:** [Project 1 — Proposal due](/assignment/project-1-proposal.html)

## Key concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Interaction** | An effect in which one predictor's slope depends on the value of another predictor. |
| **Product term** | The predictor formed by multiplying two predictors to capture their interaction. |
| **Moderation** | The phenomenon, encoded by an interaction, of one variable altering another's effect. |
| **Moderator** | The predictor whose value changes the slope of the focal predictor. |
| **Simple slope** | The effect of the focal predictor evaluated at a specific value of the moderator. |
| **Conditional (main) effect** | A predictor's slope when the other predictor in the product is zero. |
| **Centering** | Rescaling predictors around their means so conditional effects are interpretable. |
| **Interaction plot** | A graph of fitted lines that displays how a slope shifts across moderator values. |
